economic_notes: CFN Holdings LP (99.99% owned) and Cheam Enterprises Inc. (100% owned) are government business enterprises accounted for using modified equity method. CFN Holdings LP had revenue of $2,651,358 and net income of $1,447,798 for year ended December 31, 2021. Cheam Enterprises Inc. had revenue of $956 and net loss of $11,484 for year ended March 31, 2022.
notes: Audit is qualified due to exclusion of budgeted information (not a going concern issue). Two consolidated subsidiaries: Pelólxw Historical Society and CFN Housing Society. Two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: CFN Holdings LP and Cheam Enterprises Inc. Prior year included $1,000,000 settlement funds and significantly higher earnings from GBEs ($3,929,378 vs $1,436,169), explaining the large swing from $5.6M surplus in 2021 to $628K deficit in 2022. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $3,514,706 + Province of BC $602,441 + Other federal funding $163,657 + FNHA $580,168 = $4,844,972 (CMHC $64,460 not included in main revenue lines but shown in segment as part of other government). No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. Land Management segment expenses: $461,803. Taxation segment expenses: $59,715.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
